page: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8252C processor running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory and compression capabilities. The instance achieved a passmark memory score in the top 25% tier and a large-block memory write bandwidth of 97.33 GB/sec, also in the top 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression reached 31.71 GB/sec. Standard compute benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core and multi-core, fall within the average range. However, due to the lack of hardware acceleration, large-scale LLM inference is a notable tradeoff, with 7B model text generation performing weakly at 8.26 tokens/sec and 70B model prompt processing rated as poor at 5.11 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x2iezn.8xlarge offers high resource density specifically tailored for memory-bound workloads that also require high CPU clock speeds. The high RAM-to-vCPU ratio makes it highly suitable for large in-memory databases, enterprise applications, memory-intensive data processing, and electronic design automation workloads.
description: The Amazon Web Services x2iezn.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for workloads requiring high memory capacity and fast CPU clock speeds. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8252C processor running at 4.5 GHz, this instance provides 32 vCPUs and 1024.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, yielding 32.0 GB of memory per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and features a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Benchmark results show strong performance in memory write bandwidth at 97.33 GB/sec and single-threaded compression, while standard compute and LLM inference speeds remain average to weak. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning, and electronic design automation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR4 RAM, a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor, and 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance combining a high-frequency 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor with an exceptional one-terabyte DDR4 memory capacity.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs powered by a high-frequency 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8252C processor
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Strong memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, EDA, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads
